content:
type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both need to touch NDCG in the same file/class — true file-level split isn't possible. My feature adds an optional relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute() (used for both discounted gain and ideal gain, default binary 1.0 preserved). Can you describe exactly what your IDCG fix changes (which lines/logic)? Proposal: you finish+push your IDCG fix first, then I merge team/agent1 into my branch and layer the relevance_scores param on top so ideal gain also honors it. That avoids duplicate/conflicting edits.

my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into my branch, resolved the IDCG conflict by combining your ideal_length=len(expected_ids) fix with my ideal_relevances sorting. All good except one pre-existing test now fails due to your IDCG behavior change: test_metrics.py::test_ndcg Case 4 (expected_ids=[id1,id2,id3,id4], retrieved_ids=[id1,id2], mode=linear) expects 1.0 but now gets 0.6367, since IDCG is no longer capped by len(retrieved_ids). This is a consequence of your bugfix, not mine — do you want to update that test's expected value, or should I? Let me know so we don't duplicate edits to test_metrics.py.
